member.name: T:DotNet.SQLite.CrudGenerator.Attributes.CompositeKeyAttribute
member.summary: Marks a property as part of a composite primary key. Apply this attribute to two or more properties on a [SqliteCrudEntity]-decorated class to define a composite primary key instead of the default single-column Id key.

remarks.para: When at least two properties in an entity carry this attribute the generator emits a PRIMARY KEY (col1, col2, …) constraint in the CREATE TABLE statement and builds the WHERE clauses for SELECT, UPDATE, and DELETE operations accordingly.

member.name: P:DotNet.SQLite.CrudGenerator.Attributes.CompositeKeyAttribute.Order
member.summary: Gets or sets the zero-based position of this column within the composite primary key. Properties are sorted by ascending before the key definition is emitted.

member.name: T:DotNet.SQLite.CrudGenerator.BulkTransfer.BulkImportExportEngine`1
member.summary: Production-grade engine for async bulk import and export with full streaming support, configurable batching, real-time progress reporting, and optional durable checkpointing.

remarks.para: The engine implements both and through the combined interface, making it suitable for direct injection or construction via .
All public methods honour the supplied . Per-batch timeouts are enforced independently via a linked token source so that a slow batch does not prevent the operation from being cancelled cleanly.

member.typeparam: The entity type managed by this engine. Must be a reference type.

member.summary: Number of records already committed before this checkpoint was saved.

member.summary: Zero-based index of the last batch that was committed successfully.

member.name: T:DotNet.SQLite.CrudGenerator.BulkTransfer.ValidationMode
member.summary: Controls how validation failures are handled during a bulk import operation.

member.name: F:DotNet.SQLite.CrudGenerator.BulkTransfer.ValidationMode.Strict
member.summary: Reject the entire operation on the first record that fails validation.

member.name: F:DotNet.SQLite.CrudGenerator.BulkTransfer.ValidationMode.Lenient
member.summary: Skip invalid records and continue importing valid ones.

member.name: F:DotNet.SQLite.CrudGenerator.BulkTransfer.ValidationMode.None
member.summary: Disable per-record validation entirely for maximum ingestion throughput.

member.name: T:DotNet.SQLite.CrudGenerator.BulkTransfer.ImportFormat
member.summary: Serialization formats accepted by bulk import operations.

member.name: F:DotNet.SQLite.CrudGenerator.BulkTransfer.ImportFormat.Json
member.summary: JSON array or newline-delimited JSON (NDJSON).

member.name: F:DotNet.SQLite.CrudGenerator.BulkTransfer.ImportFormat.Csv
member.summary: Comma-separated values with an optional header row.

member.name: F:DotNet.SQLite.CrudGenerator.BulkTransfer.ImportFormat.Xml
member.summary: XML document with a root element wrapping individual entity elements.

member.name: T:DotNet.SQLite.CrudGenerator.BulkTransfer.BulkTransferOptions
member.summary: Configuration options that govern the behavior of . Controls batching, concurrency, progress reporting, checkpointing, and error thresholds.

member.name: P:DotNet.SQLite.CrudGenerator.BulkTransfer.BulkTransferOptions.BatchSize
member.summary: Number of entities per database write batch. Larger values reduce round-trips but increase per-failure blast radius. Default: 500.

member.name: P:DotNet.SQLite.CrudGenerator.BulkTransfer.BulkTransferOptions.MaxConcurrency
member.summary: Maximum number of concurrent batch write tasks. Increasing this value improves throughput on multi-core hosts but raises memory and connection pressure. Default: 4.

member.name: P:DotNet.SQLite.CrudGenerator.BulkTransfer.BulkTransferOptions.EnableProgressReporting
member.summary: Enables emission of snapshots during the operation. Disable for tight-loop scenarios where observer overhead is measurable. Default: true.

member.name: P:DotNet.SQLite.CrudGenerator.BulkTransfer.BulkTransferOptions.ProgressReportingInterval
member.summary: Minimum number of records processed between consecutive progress notifications. Lower values produce finer-grained feedback at higher callback overhead. Default: 100.

member.name: P:DotNet.SQLite.CrudGenerator.BulkTransfer.BulkTransferOptions.BufferSize
member.summary: Stream read/write buffer size in bytes. Larger buffers reduce I/O syscalls on sequential workloads. Default: 65 536 (64 KB).

member.name: P:DotNet.SQLite.CrudGenerator.BulkTransfer.BulkTransferOptions.EnableCheckpointing
member.summary: Persist checkpoint state to disk so that interrupted imports can be resumed from the last committed batch rather than restarted from scratch. Default: false.

member.name: P:DotNet.SQLite.CrudGenerator.BulkTransfer.BulkTransferOptions.CheckpointFilePath
member.summary: Absolute file path used to read and write checkpoint state. Required when is true.

member.name: P:DotNet.SQLite.CrudGenerator.BulkTransfer.BulkTransferOptions.ValidationMode
member.summary: Governs how per-record validation failures are handled during import. Default: .

member.name: P:DotNet.SQLite.CrudGenerator.BulkTransfer.BulkTransferOptions.MaxErrorThreshold
member.summary: Maximum cumulative validation or persistence errors before the operation is aborted. Default: 1 000.

member.name: P:DotNet.SQLite.CrudGenerator.BulkTransfer.BulkTransferOptions.UseTransactions
member.summary: When true, each batch write is wrapped in a database transaction, providing atomicity at the batch level. Default: true.

member.name: P:DotNet.SQLite.CrudGenerator.BulkTransfer.BulkTransferOptions.BatchTimeout
member.summary: Per-batch operation timeout. Batches that exceed this duration are cancelled and recorded as failures, but the overall import continues. Default: 30 seconds.

member.name: P:DotNet.SQLite.CrudGenerator.BulkTransfer.BulkTransferOptions.Default
member.summary: Returns a default options instance appropriate for most production workloads.

member.name: P:DotNet.SQLite.CrudGenerator.BulkTransfer.BulkTransferOptions.HighThroughput
member.summary: Returns an options instance tuned for maximum ingestion throughput. Increases batch size, disables per-record validation, and raises concurrency. Not recommended when data quality cannot be guaranteed.

member.name: P:DotNet.SQLite.CrudGenerator.BulkTransfer.BulkTransferOptions.Safe
member.summary: Returns an options instance optimised for safety over speed. Uses strict validation, small batches, and checkpointing enabled. Requires to be set before use.

member.name: T:DotNet.SQLite.CrudGenerator.BulkTransfer.BulkTransferOptionsExtensions
member.summary: Provides extension methods for to simplify configuration and enable common bulk transfer scenarios.

member.name: M:DotNet.SQLite.CrudGenerator.BulkTransfer.BulkTransferOptionsExtensions.WithHighPerformance(DotNet.SQLite.CrudGenerator.BulkTransfer.BulkTransferOptions,System.Nullable{System.Int32},System.Nullable{System.Int32})
member.summary: Configures the options for high-performance bulk operations with minimal overhead.
member.param: The options to configure. Cannot be .

member.name: T:DotNet.SQLite.CrudGenerator.BulkTransfer.BulkTransferPipeline`1
member.summary: Fluent pipeline builder that composes import, validation, transformation, and export stages into a single executable transfer operation backed by .

remarks.para: Pipelines are not thread-safe and must not be shared across concurrent operations. The configured stages (transform, filter, progress, error handler, retry) persist on the builder instance and apply to every subsequent execution call, making the pipeline safely reusable within the same scope.
Obtain an instance via constructor injection of or the static factory after registering bulk-transfer services with AddBulkTransfer<T>.

member.typeparam: The entity type flowing through the pipeline. Must be a reference type.

member.returns: The same pipeline instance for method chaining.

member.summary: Registers all bulk-transfer services for entity type with scoped lifetime. Subsequent calls for the same are idempotent — existing registrations are preserved.
member.remarks:
The following registrations are added (each via TryAdd*):
— the concrete, scoped implementation
resolved from the concrete registration above
resolved from the concrete registration above
resolved from the concrete registration above
— transient fluent builder
An with TKey = int must already be registered (e.g. via AddApplicationServices) before the first scope is created.
member.typeparam: The entity type to register transfer services for.
member.param: Optional delegate that mutates a fresh instance before it is captured by the engine factory. When null, is used unchanged.
member.returns: The same service collection to allow call chaining.

member.name: T:DotNet.SQLite.CrudGenerator.Configuration.DotnetSqliteCrudGeneratorOptions
member.summary: Root options class for the DotNet SQLite CRUD Generator application. This class consolidates all configuration options and provides centralized access to application settings through the IOptions pattern.
member.remarks: All configuration properties are optional with sensible defaults to ensure the application runs without requiring extensive configuration in development. Production deployments should override these defaults with appropriate values.

member.summary: Provides the configuration necessary to enable and manage soft-delete functionality within generated CRUD operations.
member.remarks: When soft-delete is enabled, DELETE operations are converted to UPDATE operations that set the specified column to the deleted value, and SELECT operations include a WHERE clause to filter out deleted records.

member.name: T:DotNet.SQLite.CrudGenerator.Services.AuditTrailService
member.summary: Persists structured audit trail entries to the AuditLogs SQLite table and provides rich querying capabilities for compliance and debugging.

remarks.para: This service writes directly to the underlying database rather than going through the generic so that it can build efficient parameterised queries without loading full entity collections into memory.
Use to capture before/after state when performing mutations, then use or the typed convenience methods to review history.
